item.title: Convox 自动化使哪些 DevOps 工程师的工作变得更容易？ | What DevOps engineer jobs does Convox automation make easier?
item.description:
成功的 DevOps 团队需要考虑很多因素。 首先，他们需要有组织的流程来简化核心服务的交付——构建、内部工具、质量检查、测试、部署和自动化。 但这种需求也为强大的 DevOps 工具创造了一个空白，以支持工程师角色并授权他们管理动态工作流。

当谈到 DevOps 工程师角色时，编码并不是该部分的全部内容。 该工作还涉及工作流和流程，例如发布到 Web 服务器、负载平衡、测试、版本控制系统等。这些流程非常耗时且需要极高的精度。 一个错误和 TTM（上市时间）显着增加。

那么，Convox 如何帮助减少花费在每项任务上的时间，同时提高运营效率呢？ 让我们来了解一下：

什么是Convox？

这为您提供了具有完全隐私和控制权的您自己的 PaaS，而无需其他平台典型的不可预测的定价结构。

开源平台
  • 与云无关，支持 AWS、Digital Ocean、Google Cloud 等。
  • 加速部署。
  • 当构建工程师推送到 GitHub 时，它会自动触发创建新的云服务和容器以进行分支测试。
  • 当工程师合并到 GitHub 时，它会自动触发将新图像和容器推出到生产环境中。
  • 该应用程序会根据负载自动放大或缩小。
  • 由于检测到故障，云服务会自动替换或丢弃云堆栈的组件。

    • 为什么选择 Convox？

    良好的 DevOps 文化可以促进在开发生命周期内执行并行开发和测试工作流。

    如今，许多企业都采用 DevOps 文化来简化工作流程、跟上市场需求、加快升级并加快云应用程序的软件交付。 但是，手动运行此过程既复杂又耗时。

    进入自动化。

    通过促进更快的软件发布，自动化有助于将 DevOps 流程的效率提升到一个全新的水平。 在工具集成的帮助下，DevOps 自动化减少了在软件开发生命周期 (SDLC) 中花费的时间，并提高了交付的精确度、一致性、可靠性和速度。

    现在，您为什么要选择 Convox 来简化您的 DevOps 任务？

    构建量身定制的系统成本高昂，并且会分散您对核心业务目标的注意力，因为您必须管理招聘、研发、维护等。

  • 市场上提供的私有解决方案基于实验软件。 这些平台也缺乏成熟度。

    • 这些问题通常需要大量的前期投资，并且从长远来看可能会为 DevOps 团队发现新问题。

    这就是 Convox 擅长的地方。

    它保留您的标准流程和任务，同时造成最小的中断。 它不仅大大节省了监控时间，而且还降低了成本、增强了资源管理等。

    以下是 Convox 如何简化 DevOps 工程师的工作：

    1. 更快的部署以帮助构建和发布工程师。 它有效地管理您的服务器、数据和网络，这意味着您只需使用一个命令即可部署和扩展应用程序。

    使用convox deploy命令在 AWS 上部署任何十二因素应用程序。 自动化工作流 使用 Convox 可以轻松设计和执行自动化工作流，它们可以帮助工程师管理应用程序的定期部署以进行登台和生产。

    以下是 Convox 如何简化交付工程师和自动化工程师的任务：

    • 对应用程序进行的每次更新都会触发可部署版本的创建
    • 滚动部署负责及时升级，零停机时间
    • 只需一个命令即可轻松回滚到以前的版本
    • 通过统一控制台为所有应用程序创建 CI/CD 管道。
    • 轻松自动部署到一个或多个云。
    • 为 GitLab 和 GitHub 提供源代码控制集成。
    • 与 Jenkins、CircleCI、GitHub Actions 等集成。

    1. 帮助 SysAdmin 进行基于角色的访问控制
      借助 Convox，系统管理员可以通过多种访问角色控制团队成员的访问级别，从而帮助细化控制并保护您的环境。 为 DevOps 工程师提供 360 度可见性，包括审计日志测试工程师和敏捷教练可以使用 Convox 来减少错误修复所需的时间或通过多种方式进行实时升级：

    • 构建工程师可以使用 convox start 启动和更改任何应用程序。
    • 使用“Convox Integrations”将补丁发送到 GitHub > CircleCI > Production > Slack Notification。
    • 使用 convox scale 来扩展服务。
    • 测试工程师可以使用 convox exec 调试实时应用程序
    • 质量工程师可以使用 convox 代理来分析应用程序的私有数据库

    这些敏捷流程需要详细的审计来跟踪团队成员的活动。 这就是审计日志的用武之地。

    1. 其“自托管”选项可帮助测试工程师无缝遵守 GDPR、PCI、HIPAA 和 SOC2 标准。

      • 在整个云环境中监控和管理您的基础架构
      • 通过一个控制台检查应用程序的运行状况和负载
      • 具有 Syslog 转发、日志聚合和指标仪表板的功能
      • 提供完成的每项更改以及由谁完成的完整审计日志
      • 检查应用程序更改的整个历史记录并单击回滚


      使用 Convox Console 简化现代 DevSecOps 工作流程 Console 比 Convox Rack 领先一步。 它带有直观的基于 Web 的工具，允许您在机架之间切换、与第三方工具集成、控制开发人员访问等。

    机架共享：机架共享允许团队使用 CLI 命令共享机架。 这对于允许或限制个别工程师访问资源至关重要。 对于精密系统，DevSecOps 工程师必须确保团队成员拥有通过 Grid 接口通过“API 密钥”进行身份验证的专有凭据。

  • 当您将代码推送到 GitHub 上的 master 时，它会触发自动构建并部署到您的 Convox 应用程序。

  • 最后一句话

    对于尚无资源和能力来处理庞大的 DevOps 工程师团队的成长型企业而言， Convox是最好的 PaaS 平台之一。 该平台为管理多个客户项目且只有少数 DevOps 工程师的公司提供了完美的解决方案。

    通过简化标准任务和工作流程的创建，Convox 加快了交付生命周期并推动及时的产品发布。 它通过自动触发部署部署、更快的回滚、现代十二因素应用程序等的单个命令来节省时间和费用。

    这主要有利于那些被平凡、重复的手动任务束缚的人——他们希望实现其核心业务目标并通过自动化交付真正的价值。 如果您想使用 Convox 让您的工作更轻松，请立即注册一个免费帐户。



    • 原文:

    Successful DevOps teams have a lot to account for. First, they need organized processes that streamline the delivery of core services - builds, internal tooling, quality checks, testing, deployments, and automation. But this need also creates a gap for powerful DevOps tools to support engineer roles and empower them to manage dynamic workflows.

    When it comes to DevOps engineer roles, coding isn't all there is to the part. The job also involves workflows and processes such as releasing to web servers, load balancing, testing, version control systems, etc. These processes are time-consuming and require utmost precision. One mistake and the TTM (time-to-market) increases significantly.

    So, how can Convox help to reduce the time spent on each task while increasing operational efficiency? Let’s find out:

    What is Convox?

    Convox is a cloud-agnostic PaaS (Platform-as-a-Service) that DevOps engineers can use to deploy applications to the cloud more efficiently. Convox streamlines the entire deployment lifecycle by integrating cloud providers' services with other open-source tools to make up a highly reliable platform. This gives you your own PaaS with total privacy and control without the unpredictable pricing structure typical of other platforms.

    Key Features of Convox:

    • Open-source platform
    • Cloud-agnostic, supporting AWS, Digital Ocean, Google Cloud, etc.
    • Accelerates deployment.
    • As the build engineer pushes to GitHub, it automatically triggers the creation of new cloud services and containers for branch testing.
    • As the engineer merges to GitHub, it automatically triggers the rollout of new images and containers toward production.
    • The app automatically scales up or down depending on the load.
    • Cloud services automatically replace or discard components of the cloud stack due to detection of failure.

    Why Choose Convox?

    DevOps is an automation culture that bridges the gap between developers and operations teams. A good DevOps culture promotes the execution of parallel development and testing workflows within the development lifecycle.

    Today, the DevOps culture is embraced by many businesses to streamline workflows, keep up with market demands, speed-up upgrades and accelerate software delivery for cloud applications. But, manually running this procedure is complicated and time-consuming.

    Enter automation.

    Automation helps to take the efficacy of DevOps processes to a whole new level by facilitating faster software releases. With the help of tool integrations, DevOps automation reduces the time spent in the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and allows for more precision, consistency, reliability, and acceleration in deliveries.

    Now, why should you choose Convox to streamline your DevOps tasks?

    Convox solves two of the main bottlenecks that arise within the internal tooling of businesses:

    1. Building a tailored system is costly and distracts you from core business goals, as you have to manage hiring, R & D, maintenance, and so on.
    2. Private solutions offered in the market are based on experimental software. These platforms also lack maturity.

    These problems often demand substantial upfront investments and may uncover new issues in the long run for the DevOps team.

    Here's where Convox excels.

    Convox replaces lengthy and complex code development processes and simplifies various DevOps engineer jobs by automating repetitive tasks related to code deployment, testing, upgrades, maintenance, and more.

    What DevOps Engineer jobs does Convox make easier?

    Convox is a PaaS solution that addresses DevOps pain points and vitally reduces time spent managing cloud resources. It retains your standard processes and tasks while causing minimal disruption. Not only does it substantially save the number of hours spent in monitoring, but it also reduces costs, enhances resource management, and more.

    Here’s how Convox simplifies DevOps Engineer jobs:

    1. Faster deploys to aid the build and release engineers. Convox can install ‘’Convox Rack”- a power-packed PaaS into your cloud account in just a few minutes. It efficiently manages your servers, data, and networks, which means that you deploy and scale applications using just one command.

    The convox install command will configure a production-ready infrastructure with the best and latest AWS services for your applications. EC2, ASG, CloudFormation, S3, Lambda, ELB, VPC, etc., play a vital role within the system. Convox manages the tasks of testing, researching, and integrating all of these services, thereby reducing complexity.

    Use convox deploy command to deploy any twelve-factor application upon AWS. Convox leverages build and release API to create images, load balancers, and containers.

    1. Automated Workflows Automated workflows are easy to design and execute with Convox, and they help engineers manage the regular deployment of applications to staging and production.

    Here’s how Convox simplifies the delivery engineer and automation engineer’s tasks:

    • Every update made to the application triggers the creation of a deployable release
    • Rolling deploys take care of timely upgrades with zero downtime
    • Easy rollbacks to a previous version using just a single command
    • Creates CI/CD pipelines for all applications via a unified console.
    • Easily automate deployments to one or more clouds.
    • Offers source control integrations for GitLab and GitHub.
    • Integrates with Jenkins, CircleCI, GitHub Actions, etc.

    1. Helps SysAdmin with Role-based Access Control
      With Convox, SysAdmins can control team members’ access levels with multiple access roles that help granulize control and secure your environments. Convox manages this seamlessly by leveraging hardware two Factor-Authentication (2FA).

      1. Aids DevOps Engineers with 360-degree visibility including audit logging Test engineers and agile coaches can use Convox to reduce the time taken for bug fixes or to get upgrades live in several ways:
    • Build engineers can start and change any application with convox start.
    • Use ‘Convox Integrations’ to ship patches to GitHub > CircleCI > Production > Slack Notification.
    • Use convox scale to scale a service.
    • DevSecOps engineers can use convox rack updates to apply infrastructure security updates across the board.
    • Test engineers can use convox exec to debug the live app
    • Quality engineers can use convox proxy to analyze an application’s private database

    These agile processes call for detailed audits to track team members’ activities. This is where the Audit Log comes in.

    1. Convox creates detailed logs of all the changes made to the applications to maintain compliance requirements.

    Its ‘self-hosted’ option helps test engineers seamlessly comply with the GDPR, PCI, HIPAA, and SOC2 standards.

    • Monitor and manage your infrastructure throughout cloud environments
    • Check health and load of the applications via one console
    • Features Syslog Forwarding, Log Aggregation, and Metrics Dashboard
    • Provides a complete audit log of every change done and by whom
    • Check the entire history of application changes and roll back with a click


    Convox also provides engineers with a single API to access or upgrade resources and keep track of all API calls, even the sensitive ones.

    1. Use Convox Console to streamline modern DevSecOps workflows Console goes one step ahead of the Convox Rack. It comes with intuitive, web-based tools that allow you to switch between Racks, integrate with third-party tools, control developer access, etc.

    Convox Console comprises three core features, namely:

    • Rack Sharing: Rack Sharing allows teams to share Racks using CLI commands. This is vital for allowing or restricting access of individual engineers to resources. For delicate systems, DevSecOps engineers must ensure that team members have exclusive credentials with authentication via an ‘API key’ through Grid’s interface.

    • GitHub Integration: Console seamlessly integrates with GitHub to simplify the setup of CI/CD workflows. As you push code to master on GitHub, it triggers automatic building and deploys to your Convox app.

    • Slack Integration: Businesses often leverage robust chat services like Slack for seamless collaboration amongst DevOps teams. Grid offers an ‘Add to Slack’ button, where engineers can opt-in to get critical notifications about specific Racks.

    Final Word

    Convox is one of the best PaaS platforms for growing businesses that do not yet have the resources and capacity to handle a large team of DevOps engineers. The platform offers a perfect solution for companies that manage multiple client projects with a limited number of DevOps engineers onboard.

    By simplifying the creation of standard tasks and workflows, Convox speeds up the delivery lifecycle and drives timely product releases. It saves time and expenses with single commands that automatically trigger rollout deployments, faster rollbacks, modern twelve-factor apps, etc.

    Convox helps businesses gain operational efficiency, with a limited pool of DevOps engineers. This primarily benefits those chained by mundane, repetitive manual tasks – who desire to reach their core business goals and deliver true value via automation. If you want to to use Convox to make your job easier, register for a free account today.

item.title: 设计模式（二）——工厂模式 | Design pattern(II) - Factory pattern
item.description:
在上一篇文章中，我分享了为什么我们需要设计模式和工厂模式的基本概念。

我们使用工厂模式来创建具有相同特征的对象，上一篇文章中的示例

class User { constructor ( name , age , career ) { this . name = name this . age = age this . career = career } } function Factory ( name , age , career ) { let job switch ( career ) { case " programmer " : job = [ ' xxx ' , ' yyy ' , ' zzz ' ] break ; case " accounting " : job = [ ' abc ' , ' def ' , ' jkm ' ] break ; ... } return new User ( name , age , career , job ) }

有一个明显的缺陷。 如果有数百种职业类型，其中一些是C级（CEO、CTO、CXO），那么他们的工作和权限就大不相同。 您可能会想我们如何才能更轻松地创建不同的用户？ 也许我们需要一个蓝图并用它来创建一个抽象工厂。 所以，每个真正的用户都是由抽象工厂制造的……

是的！ 这就是今天的主要课程，抽象。

我们知道每个员工都有自己的姓名、年龄、职业……他们有权访问公司的系统。 员工可以查看自己的信息。 人力资源人员可以看到所有员工的考勤记录，而总经理可能拥有越来越多的权限。

所以，在我们的抽象工厂（UserFactory）

 class UserFactory { constructor ( name , age , career ) { this . name = name this . age = age this . career = career } getPermission () { console . log ( " I am abstract factory " ) } }

我们不会使用UserFactory ，而是使用它来创建具体的工厂，我们可以指定一个具有特定权限的用户

class Programmer extends UserFactory { constructor ( name , age , career ) { super ( name , age , career ) } getPermission () { return new labPermission () } }
 class Permission { checkIdentity () { console . log ( " I am abstract product " ) } } class labPermission extends Permission { checkIdentity () { console . log ( " Identity checked, programmer " ) } }

因此，如果程序员想要访问实验室，他/她可以做到

const programmerOne = new Programmer () //create programmer instance const programmerPermission = programmerOne . getPermission () programmerPermission . checkIdentity () //Identity checked, programmer

所以，有一天，如果新会计想创建用户的权限，我们只需要从UserFactory扩展类。 我们不修改它，也不用担心上面提到的问题（ Factory变得过于庞大和复杂而无法维护。）

概括

无论是抽象工厂还是工厂方法，他们都试图将系统的不变部分和可变部分解耦。

抽象工厂有四个关键要素

  1. 抽象工厂：它是一个蓝图，用于定义对象的外观，在我们的例子中是UserFactory 。

  2. 具体工厂：它继承了抽象工厂的方法和特性。 我们用它来创建一个真实的对象，在我们的例子中是Programmer 。

  3. 抽象产品：在我们的例子中，抽象产品是我们的Permission ，因为我们使用它来创建真正的权限。 和抽象工厂一样，我们不使用它来new或创建一个真实的对象。

  4. 具体产品：如labPermission 。 这是对程序员的真正许可。

感谢您的时间。 如果您有任何反馈，欢迎在下方发表评论。



原文:

In the previous post, I share the very basic concept of why we need the design pattern and the fundamental of the factory pattern.

We use the factory pattern to create objects that have same features, the example in the previous post

class User { constructor(name, age, career) { this.name = name this.age = age this.career = career }
} function Factory(name, age, career) { let job switch(career) { case "programmer": job = ['xxx', 'yyy', 'zzz'] break; case "accounting": job = ['abc', 'def', 'jkm'] break; ... } return new User(name, age, career, job)
}

There is an obvious defect. If there are hundreds of career's type and some of them are C-class (CEO, CTO, CXO), so they have very different job and permission. Factory would become too huge and complex to maintain.

You may think how we can create different users easier? Maybe we need a blueprint and use it to create an abstract factory. So, every real user is made from the abstract factory...

YES! That is today's main course, abstraction.

We know every employee has their name, age, career...and they have their permission to access a company's system. A employee can see own information. A human resource staff can see all employee's attendance records and A general manager may have more and more permission.

**But, even there are so much different type of employees, they are the user of the system.

So, in our abstract factory (UserFactory)

class UserFactory { constructor(name, age, career) { this.name = name this.age = age this.career = career } getPermission() { console.log("I am abstract factory") }
}

We are not going to use UserFactory, instead we use it to create concrete factories and we can specify a user that having a specific permission

class Programmer extends UserFactory { constructor(name, age, career) { super(name, age, career) } getPermission() { return new labPermission() }
}

class Permission { checkIdentity() { console.log("I am abstract product") }
} class labPermission extends Permission { checkIdentity() { console.log("Identity checked, programmer") }
}

So, if a programmer wants to access to the lab, he/she can do

const programmerOne = new Programmer()
//create programmer instance const programmerPermission = programmerOne.getPermission() programmerPermission.checkIdentity()
//Identity checked, programmer

So, one day, if new accountant would like to create user's permission, only we need to do is to extend the class from the UserFactory. We do not revise it and not to worry about the problem mentioned above (Factory becomes too huge and complex to maintain.)

Summary

No matter the abstract factory or factory method, they are trying to decouple the invariable and variable part of a system.

There are four key elements in abstraction factory

  1. Abstract factory: It is a blueprint and used to defined what an object looks like, UserFactory in our case.

  2. Concrete factory: It inherits methods and features from the abstract factory. We use it to create a real object, Programmer in our case.

  3. Abstract product: In our case, the abstract product is our Permission because we use it to create a real permission. And it is same to the abstract factory, we do not use it to new or create a real object.

  4. Concrete product: Like labPermission. It is a real permission for programmer.

Thank you for your time. If you have any feedbacks, welcome to comment below.
item.author: isoscar

item.title: CSS中的纵横比 | aspect-ratio in css
item.description:
阿里-埃尔德巴

发表于例如，您的网站上有一个视频，它在移动媒体中的尺寸是16:9 ，您必须调整它的大小，所以它可能会丢失它的尺寸，所以我们可以这样做
video { aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; }
现在您可以根据需要调整它的大小！

我的问题

item.title: 我的第一个博客 - 如何写 8log🙂🙂 | My first Blog - How to write 8log🙂🙂
item.description:
它对初学者如何做以及如何做和克服你的错误有很大帮助。

当今世界🌎的进步超出了我们的想象。 对这个世界的一点点贡献，就是向前迈出的一大步。 做这种事情的最好方法是写博客，所以把你的手指放在关键字上，然后写。

这也是我第一个给你知识的博客如何写博客？ 听起来很奇怪😄。
但是给你一点知识，这有助于你听起来更好🙂。

我不会给你最好的建议并且会犯很多错误，你会笑😂，你会避免我犯的错误。 这就是我想要的。 通过这种方式，错误作为知识传递。 这就是博客的魅力🙂。

在我的博客之旅中，我将遵循一些技巧。 他们是：

开始写作是最艰难的，让它尽可能简单。 我也犹豫着写，但克服了这种恐惧，我开始了它。 老实说，如果你开始写博客，你会在你的博客结束时停下来。 当大脑停止思考要写什么时，您会停下来，然后您必须休息一下并自动放松，新的想法将作为您博客的词而诞生。

我们应该以描述性和有用的方式命名您的博客，以使读者吸引您的博客。

总是写得独一无二。 而不是抄袭其他写的简单，感觉这个博客是我写的。 从其他内容创作者那里获得灵感，但不要忘记你是谁，你是谁！

假设你是读者，想🧐你想从这个博客中得到什么并写下来。
找到错误有很大帮助，您可以轻松地重构它。

写博客是当我们一次又一次地写博客时提高的技能。 当您保持一致时，它会提高您的生产力、视角和信心

在博客中。 分享我的看法，这也是我的第一个博客我不知道将来会发生什么，但今天有做某事的意愿。



原文:

Blogging is the best way to share your skill, experience, knowledge, mistake and many other things you have been learned throughout your journey to the new comers in your field. It help a lot to the beginners what to do and how to do and overcome you mistake.

Today's world🌎 is advancing beyond we think. Small contribution to this world make one step far in advancement. The best way to do such thing is Blogging so place your finger in keyword and just write.

This is also my first blog giving you the knowledge How to write Blog?? sound weird😄.
But Giving you little much knowledge which helps you little bit that's sound better🙂.

I wouldn't give you the best tip and do many mistake and You laugh😂 on it and you avoid mistake done by me. That's the thing I want. In this way, Mistake is passed as knowledge. This is the beauty of Blogging🙂.

There are some tips that I will follow throughout my blogging journey. They are:

1. Just start to write anything that you think

Starting to write is toughest, make it as easy as possible. I also hesitate to write but overcoming this fear I started it. To be honest, If you started to write blog , you will stop at the end of your blog. Something you will stop when brain stop to think what to write then you must take break and relaxed automatically new ideas will born as the word for your blog.

2. Title is everything

We should name your blog in a descriptive and helpful way which makes reader to attract towards your blog.

3. Unique voice

Always be unique to write. Rather than copying other write simple which makes feeling of This blog is written by me. Get inspired by other content creator but don't forget what you are and who you are!

4. Try to read your blog as reader

Assume that you are reader and think🧐 what you want from this blog and write it.
It help a lot to find the mistake and you can easily refactor it.

5. Consistently in blogging

Blogging is the skill which is improved when we write blog again and again. It increases your productivity, perspective and confidence when you are consistent

in blogging. Sharing my opinion, This is also my first blog I don't know what will be in future but there is a will to do something today.

item.title: Django 中的复杂数据类型 | Complex datatypes in Django
item.description:
安娜威廉

发表于我必须展示人们可以用钥匙打开建筑物中的哪些门。 我必须通过 4 张桌子才能做到这一点。 所以我的选择是：

  1. 创建对 StoredProcedures 的调用，让数据库引擎更快地完成它。
  2. 创建数据库视图并让数据库更快地完成它。
  3. 其他想法？

我们已经在数据库中拥有并使用了 Triggers、StoredProcedures 和 Views，因此没有先见之明的优先级来以任何特定的方式做事。



原文:

Ana Willem

Posted on Jun 2

I have to show which doors in a building people can open with their keys. There are 4 tables I have to get through to do it. The ORM is just not cutting through it (or it does but it takes a minute for some folks with master keys to load all of the doors).

So my options are:

  1. Create calls to StoredProcedures and let the DB engine do it's thing faster.
  2. Create database views and let the DB do it's thing faster.
  3. Other ideas?

We already have and use Triggers, StoredProcedures and Views in the database, so there is no prescient precedence to do things any particular way.
item.author: jellobrain

item.title: 重构你的简历 | Refactor Your Resume
item.description:
这主要针对在校外寻找早期职业编程工作的人。

很多朋友向我寻求简历方面的帮助。 我还发现自己在很多 Discord 小组中为相同的东西提供建议，所以我决定把它写下来。

技能部分

更喜欢将职位描述中存在的技能放在首位。 不要把你几乎没有使用过的所有东西都放在这里。 展示你有足够信心接受面试的最佳技能。

我见过的几类不需要的东西：

  • 部署工具（例如：Vercel、Netlify、Heroku）：很多这些平台都有单击部署。 除了您可以单击几个按钮并编写一些配置之外，这并没有说明什么。
  • 版本控制（例如：git、Github）：虽然这些工具非常有用，但我假设您知道如何使用它并且不会通过电子邮件在 zip 存档中发送代码。
  • 没什么特别的。
  • 生产力工具（例如：Notion、Jira）：使用文本编辑器，任务管理应用程序并不是会给招聘人员留下深刻印象的技能。
  • 项目管理流行语（例如：敏捷、Scrum、瀑布）：你不是项目经理。 这些技能无关紧要。 无论您是一次性完成工作还是在冲刺中完成工作都没有关系。
  • 对于使用 SQL 的人：无需在简历中列出每个 SQL DB（MySQL、PostgreSQL、MS SQL 等）。 面试的重点是您对关系数据库模型的理解，而不是您使用的 SQL 风格。 列出一个就足够了。
  • 看到“C、C++、JavaScript、TypeScript、Python、Java、php”都是因为你用过几次看起来不太好。

不必要的部分

认证

工作证明总是比一张纸说你学到了一些东西要好。

  • 有一些角色偶尔会提到认证（ sysadmin 、 devops 、 security ）。 除此之外，在简历上添加认证并不重要。
  • 大多数在线课程都会有某种顶点项目。 构建这些项目。 添加您自己的一些功能并列出它们而不是证书。

不相关的兴趣

很可能有人会在你的面试中询问你的爱好。 说你喜欢“骑自行车或跳舞”并不会让你比其他简历更有优势。 除非你的兴趣是相关的，否则最好将它们排除在简历之外。

更多建议

避免长段落
你的简历不是英语课的论文。 保持简洁并使用要点。

如果您的经验较少，请使用单列布局
最好精简你的简历并填写一个单栏布局，而不是用不必要的东西让它膨胀。

关于教育科

  • 如果你的 GPA 很低，就把它删掉。
  • 大学之前的任何事情都与本节无关。 无需填写高中名称或大学入学考试成绩。

注意：我没有提到的每一点都有例外，因为它们非常具体。 我们可以在评论中讨论它们。

祝你找工作好运。 👍 👍



原文:

This is mainly focused on people looking for early career programming jobs off-campus.

A lot of friends reach out to me for help with their resume. I also found myself advising the same stuff on a lot of Discord groups so I decided to write it up.

The Skills section

Prefer putting skills that are present in the Job Description first. Don't put everything you've barely worked with here. Showcase your best skills that you're confident enough in to be interviewed on.

A few categories of things that I've seen which are not required:

  • Deployment Tools (eg: Vercel, Netlify, Heroku): A lot of these platforms have single-click deploys. Having this says nothing apart from you being able to click a few buttons and write a bit of configuration.
  • Version Control (eg: git, Github): While these tools are very useful, I'd assume you know how to use this & aren't sending code through email in zip archives.
  • IDEs (eg: VSCode, Sublime Text): So, you can open up a text file in a program made to do that? Nothing special about it.
  • Productivity Tools (eg: Notion, Jira): Using text editors, task management apps are not skills that are going to impress a recruiter.
  • Project Management Buzzwords (eg: Agile, Scrum, Waterfall): You're not a project manager. These skills aren't relevant. It doesn't matter if you delivered work all at once or in sprints.
  • For folks putting SQL : There's no need to list every single SQL DB (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MS SQL etc.) in your resume. Interviews focus on your understanding of the relational DB model and not what flavor of SQL you use. Listing one is enough.
  • Long List of Programming Languages : Only list languages that you've a strong grasp over. Seeing "C, C++, JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, php" all because you used them a few times doesn't look good.

Unnecessary Sections

Certifications

Proof of Work is always better than a piece of paper saying you learnt something.

  • There a few roles that occasionally mention certifications (sysadmin, devops, security). Apart from those, putting a certification on the resume doesn't matter much.
  • Most online courses will have some sort of a capstone project. Build those projects. Add a few features of your own and list them instead of the certificate.

Unrelated Interests

It's likely that someone will ask you about your hobbies in your interview. Saying that you like "cycling or dancing" doesn't give you any edge over other resumes. Unless you're interests are relevant, it's best to leave them out of the resume.

Some More Advice

Avoid Long Paragraphs
Your resume is not an essay for English class. Keep it concise and use bullet points.

Use a Single Column Layout if you've less Experience
It's better to trim down your resume and fill a single column layout than bloating it up with unnecessary things.

On the Education Section

  • If your GPA is low, remove it.
  • Anything before college isn't really relevant in this section. There's no need to put the name of your high-school or your college entrance test scores.

Note: There are exceptions to every point which I haven't mentioned since they're very specific. We can discuss them in comments.

Best of luck on your job hunt. 👍
item.author: tinfoil_knight

item.title: 了解智威汤逊 | Understanding JWT
item.description:
它是一种行业标准，用于在客户端和服务之间以及服务之间传递用户声明。 声明只是服务器验证身份和分配适当角色所需的用户信息。
一个 JWT 由 3 个部分组成

标题

The Header in JWT indicates that it is a JWT token and identifies which algorithm is used to generate the signature. JWT 中的 Header 表示它是一个 JWT 令牌，并标识使用哪种算法来生成签名。

 { "alg" : "HS256" , "typ" : "JWT"
}

有效载荷

有效负载包含一组声明。

 { "sub" : "1234567890" , "name" : "John Doe" , "iat" : 1516239022
}

签名

签名验证 JWT 令牌。 签名是通过对标头和有效负载以及秘密进行编码并将两者与句点分隔符连接在一起来生成的。

 HMAC_SHA 256 ( secret, base 64 urlEncoding(header) + '.' + base 64 urlEncoding(payload)
)

这三个部分使用句点分隔符进行编码和组合以形成 JWT 令牌。 这就是示例 JWT 的样子。 为什么选择智威汤逊

我们使用的大多数应用程序都需要我们登录才能访问一些附加功能。 通常用户通过提供他的凭据登录。 在用户会话的整个生命周期内使用用户提供的凭据并不是一个好习惯。
传统上，用户状态和相关信息由服务器维护。 他们会将这些信息存储在像 Redis 这样的外部存储中。 会话 cookie 用于从外部存储中获取用户数据。

这个解决方案仍然有效，但现代应用程序依赖于 RESTful 服务，而 RESTful 服务是无状态的。 它们不存储任何状态信息。 这使它们具有高度可扩展性。 所以需要 JWT。 服务器会将此 JWT 令牌发送给客户端，客户端必须在每个后续请求中发送此令牌。

使用 JWT 的优缺点是什么

使用 JWT 的优点

  • 由于令牌包含所有必要的信息，您不必一直查询数据库
  • 服务器不需要维护外部存储来处理用户状态
  • 易于跨多个服务进行验证。 您只有一台服务器进行授权，然后将令牌传递给需要信息的多个服务

使用 JWT 的缺点

  • 如果有人获得了 JWT，他们可能会冒充这个人
  • 您不能将大量信息存储到 JWT 令牌中，因为它会造成数据过载。 建议仅存储基本信息，根据需要通过查询数据库获取其他数据。 同样对于 Web 应用程序，您将使用带有 cookie 的 JWT，只能存储特定字节的数据。

保护您的 JWT

如上所述，如果某些恶意用户访问了您的 JWT，他们可能会冒充您的用户。 所以在使用 JWT 时一定要小心。

  • 切勿将 JWT 存储在浏览器的本地存储中。 任何恶意 javascript 代码都可以轻松访问它。
  • 如果可能，请创建寿命较短的 JWT。 但请注意，如果您使 JWT 无效，您的用户将被视为已注销。 您不希望您的用户一直在登录。
  • 确保您使用 SSL，因为它会加密您的网络流量，这将防止您的 JWT 被任何有权访问您网络的人窃取。

如果您丢失了 JWT，该怎么办

受损的 JWT 与受损的密码相同。 您应该遵循与破解密码相同的程序。 这可能包括

  • 阻止用户帐户
  • 要求用户立即更改密码
  • 撤销用户的所有权限

如何知道您是否丢失了 JWT

对受损的 JWT 快速采取行动对于防止客户数据丢失至关重要。 我们可以执行以下操作来了解我们是否丢失了 JWT

  • 监控客户端位置。 最有可能的是，如果某人破坏了您的安全，他会尝试从其他位置使用令牌，而不是您的客户正在使用的位置。 这可以很好地指示您的 JWT 是否被盗。 但是屏蔽位置是一项简单的任务。
  • 分析用户的模式。 如果您的攻击者窃取了您的 JWT，他会尽快尝试使用它。 因此，您必须检查 JWT 的行为。 例如，如果一个通常每分钟发送 10 个请求的用户开始发送 50 个请求，那么这就是 JWT 丢失的标志。

如您所见，保护 JWT 本身就是一项复杂的任务，如果您关心用户的数据，则需要付出巨大的努力。 这就是为什么总是建议您将处理身份验证的任务委托给外部服务，如 Auth0 或 Okta 或 FusionAuth。 这些服务只收取象征性费用，即可解决您的所有身份验证问题。



原文:

JWT stands for JSON web tokens. It is an industry standard, for passing user claims between client and service and between services. The claims in a JWT are encoded as a JSON object. A claim is just an information of the user which is needed by the server to verify there identity and assign appropriate roles.
A JWT consists of 3 parts

Header

The Header in JWT indicates that it is a JWT token and identifies which algorithm is used to generate the signature.

{ "alg": "HS256", "typ": "JWT"
}

Payload

The payload contains a set of claims.

{ "sub": "1234567890", "name": "John Doe", "iat": 1516239022
}

Signature

The signature validates the JWT token. The signature is generated by encoding the header and the payload along with a secret and concatenating the two together with a period separator.

HMAC_SHA256( secret, base64urlEncoding(header) + '.' + base64urlEncoding(payload)
)

The three parts are encoded and combined using a period separator to form a JWT token. This is what a sample JWT looks like. (The red part indicates the Header, the purple indicates the Payload and the blue indicates the Signature)

Why JWT

Most of the application we use requires us to be logged in to access some additional functionality. Normally user logs in by giving his credentials. It is not good practice to use the credentials provided by user through out the life time of users session.
Traditionally, the users state and related information was maintained by servers. They would store these information's in an external storage like Redis. A session cookie was used to get the users data from the external storage.

This solution would still work, but modern applications rely on RESTful services and RESTful services are stateless. They don't store any state information in them. This makes them highly scalable. So JWT was needed. JWT can store the required state level information in them in a somewhat secure manner. The server will send this JWT token to the client and the client has to send this token in every subsequent request.

What are the pros and cons of using JWT

Pros of using JWT

  • Since the tokens contain all the necessary information, you do not have to query the DB all the time
  • The server does not need to maintain an external storage to handle users state
  • Easy to validate across multiple services. You just have one server which does the authorization and then pass the token across multiple services that require the information

Cons of using JWT

  • If someone gets the JWT, they may be able to impersonate the person
  • You cannot store lots of information into the JWT token as it will create a data overload. It is advised to store only the basic information and fetch other data by querying DB as required. Also for web applications, you will use JWT with cookies can store only a certain bytes of data.

Securing your JWT

As mentioned above, if some malicious user gets access of your JWT, they might be able to impersonate your user. So you must be careful when using a JWT.

  • Never store JWT in local storage in browser. Any malicious javascript code can easily access this.
  • If possible, create JWTs with short life. But be careful, if you invalidate your JWT, your users will be treated as logged off. You don't want your users to be logging in all the time.
  • Make sure you use SSL as it will encrypt your network traffic which will prevent your JWT from being stolen by anyone who has access to your network.

What to do if you loose your JWT

A compromised JWT is same as a compromised password. You should follow the same procedure as you do for a compromised password. This might include

  • Blocking the users account
  • Asking the user to change his password immediately
  • Revoking all permissions from the user

How to know if you have lost a JWT

Acting quickly on a compromised JWT can be vital in preventing the loss of your customer data. We can do the following to know if we have lost a JWT

  • Monitor the client location. Most likely, if the someone has breached your security, he will try to use the token from some other location than the one which your client was using. This can be a good indicator to know that your JWT is stolen. But masking location is a simple task.
  • Analyze the pattern of the user. If your attacker has stolen your JWT, he will try to make use of it as soon as possible. So you will have to check the behavior of the JWTs. For example if a user who normally sends 10 requests per minutes, starts sending 50 requests, than that is a sign of lost JWT.

As you see, securing a JWT is a complex task in itself and would require a significant effort if you care about the data of your users. That's why it is always advised that you delegate the task of handling authentication to an external service like Auth0 or Okta or FusionAuth. For a nominal charge, these services will take care of all your authentication problems.
item.author: _mohanmurali

item.title: 评估流程是否可以自动化的 4 个步骤 | 4 Steps to evaluate if a process can be automated
item.description:
俾斯麦塞尔达

发表于有很多标准需要考虑，但让我们保持简单，并尝试通过 4 个步骤来解决。

异常率要么很低，要么也可以包含在业务逻辑中。

手动且非重复：流程步骤由人工执行，每次执行流程时都可能不同。

  • 手动和重复：流程中的步骤由用户执行，并且每次至少有一部分是相同的。

  • 半自动化和重复：一些重复的步骤已经自动化（使用宏、Outlook 规则等）。

  • 自动化：有些流程已经使用 RPA 以外的其他技术实现自动化。

    • 由于高异常率或无法集成到业务逻辑中的因素，需要保持手动或非重复性的流程不适合自动化。

    屏幕数量：RPA 通过对机器人进行编程以在屏幕级别执行任务（当屏幕更改时，必须教导逻辑）来工作。 屏幕数量越多，在过程自动化之前必须捕获和配置的元素就越多。

  • 应用程序类型：一些应用程序更容易自动化（例如 Office 套件或浏览器），而其他应用程序则大大增加了自动化工作量（例如大型机）。 并且不同的应用程序越多，屏幕的数量也会增加（见前一点）。

  • 业务逻辑场景：自动化的复杂性随着业务逻辑中决策点的数量而增加。 基本上，每一个都可以乘以场景数量的两倍。

  • 输入的类型和数量：如前所述，标准输入是可取的。 然而，在某些情况下，必须为每个将受到自动化影响的供应商配置一个标准输入（例如发票）。 此外，非标准输入可以具有不同的复杂程度，自由文本是最复杂的。

    • 通过使用这 4 个步骤来评估流程，我们可以了解自动化的潜力和复杂性。



    原文:

    Bismarck Cerda

    Posted on Jun 2

    An RPA developer must know how to quickly identify if a process fits for automation.

    There are to many sets of criteria to take into account but let's keep it simple and try to figure it out with 4 steps.

    1. Rule based: The decision made (including the data interpretation) in the process can be captured in a pre-defined logic. The exception rate is either low or can be included as well in the business logic.

    2. Repetitive Process: We can find 4 types of processes

    • Manual and non-repetitive: the process steps are performed by humans and can be different every time the process is executed.

    • Manual and repetitive: the steps in the process are performed by the user, and at least some of them are the same every time.

    • Semi-automated & repetitive: some of the repetitive steps have already been automated (using macros, Outlook rules, and so on).

    • Automated: there are processes that have been already automated using other technologies than RPA.

    Processes that need to stay manual or are non-repetitive, due to the high exception rate or factors that cannot be integrated in a business logic, aren't good candidates for automation.

    3. Standard Input: This set of criteria determines how hard it is to automate a process:

    • Number of Screens: RPA works by programming the robot to perform tasks at screen level (when the screen changes, the logic has to be taught). The higher the number of screens, the more elements have to be captured and configured prior to the process automation.

    • Types of applications: Some applications are more easily automated (such as the Office suite or browsers), others heavily increase the automation effort (Mainframe, for example). And the more different applications there are, the number of screens will increase, as well (see previous point).

    • Business Logic Scenarios: An automation's complexity increases with the number of decision points in the business logic. Basically, each one could multiply by two times the number of scenarios.

    • Types and numbers of inputs: As previously stated, standard input is desirable. Yet there are cases in which one standard input (such as an invoice) has to be configured for each supplier that will be affected by the automation. Moreover, non-standard input can be of different complexity grades, with free text being the most complex.

    4. Stable: Processes that have been the same for a certain period of time and no changes are expected within the next months are good candidates for automation, provided they meet the other criteria as well.

    By using these 4 steps to evaluate processes we can have an idea of the automation potential and complexity.
item.author: cerda_bismarck

item.title: 定时密码屏幕是一种欺凌仪式 | Timed code screen is a hazing ritual
item.description:
没有合理的理由使用定时代码筛选来评估工程师。

我认为其余使用这种筛选技术的公司要么将其用作智商测试，要么用作衡量编程技能的粗略捷径。 我无法想象有人真的相信它对任何一个目的都有效。 因此，公司保留这些的唯一剩余原因是继续工程师以前经历过的欺凌仪式。



原文:

There is no rational reason to evaluate an engineer using a timed code screen.

I think the remaining companies using this screening technique are using it as either an IQ test, or as a crude shortcut for measuring programming skill. I can't imagine someone actually believing it is effective for either purpose. So, the only remaining reason that companies are keeping these around is to continue a hazing ritual that engineers have experienced before.
item.author: kanishka

item.title: LeetCode :- 用解决方案实现 strStr() | LeetCode :-Implement strStr() with Solution
item.description:
罗汉卡达姆

发表于你好开发社区

问题陈述

实现 strStr()。

给定两个字符串 needle 和 haystack，返回 haystack 中第一次出现 needle 的索引，如果 needle 不是 haystack 的一部分，则返回 -1。

澄清：

当 needle 为空字符串时，我们应该返回什么？ 这是面试时问的好问题。

为了解决这个问题，当 needle 为空字符串时，我们将返回 0。 这与 C 的 strstr() 和 Java 的 indexOf() 一致。

示例 1：

输入：haystack = "hello", needle = "ll" 输出：2

示例 2：

输入： haystack = "aaaaa", needle = "bba"
输出：-1

约束：

解决方案

class Solution { public int strStr(String haystack, String needle) { return haystack.indexOf(needle); } }

在以下链接中查看更多关于我的信息：-
Rohan Ravindra Kadam Website Rohan Ravindra Kadam 网站
Rohan Ravindra Kadam Blogs Rohan Ravindra Kadam 博客
Instagram Instagram



原文:

ROHAN KADAM

Posted on Jun 2

Hello DevCommunity

Problem Statement

Implement strStr().

Given two strings needle and haystack, return the index of the first occurrence of needle in haystack, or -1 if needle is not part of haystack.

Clarification:

What should we return when needle is an empty string? This is a great question to ask during an interview.

For the purpose of this problem, we will return 0 when needle is an empty string. This is consistent to C's strstr() and Java's indexOf().

Example 1:

Input: haystack = "hello", needle = "ll" Output: 2

Example 2:

Input: haystack = "aaaaa", needle = "bba"
Output: -1

Constraints:

1 <= haystack.length, needle.length <= 104
haystack and needle consist of only lowercase English characters.

Solution 

class Solution {
    public int strStr(String haystack, String needle) {
        return haystack.indexOf(needle);
    }
}

Rohan Ravindra Kadam Website
Rohan Ravindra Kadam Blogs

item.title: Web3 Solidity + JavaScript 32 小时课程回顾 #6 | Web3 Solidity + JavaScript 32 hour course recap #6
item.description:
时间戳 1:58:55-2:23:32

由于管理能力差，我昨天睡后开始了每天的课程。

真可惜，因为那时事情开始变得有趣，我写了我的第一个 Solidity 合同！

这也是我说的其中一件事，“好吧，我会在第一次广告插播后停下来。” 然后整个小时都没有广告。

因此，我们在浏览器中使用了Remix IDE 。 虽然我下载了 VS 代码的 remix 扩展，但我无法弄清楚如何在本地实际编译或运行它。

我们花了一些时间检查类型，并创建了我们的第一个函数。

There are a couple of important tabs in Remix, but overall this was an engaging and important study session! Remix 中有几个重要的标签，但总的来说，这是一个引人入胜且重要的学习课程！



原文:

timestamp 1:58:55-2:23:32

Due to poor management skills, I wound up starting my daily lessons after my bedtime yesterday.

Which is a shame because this was when things were starting to get interesting and I wrote my first Solidity contract!

It was also one of those things where I said, "Well, I will stop after the first commercial break." and then there wasn't a commercial for the entire hour.

So, we used the Remix IDE in the browser. Although I downloaded the remix extension for VS code, but I couldn't figure out how to actually compile or run it locally.

We spent some time going over types, and made our first function.

There are a couple of important tabs in Remix, but overall this was an engaging and important study session!

item.title: 拥有多家供应商的美杜莎店面 | Medusa storefront with multiple vendors
item.description:
我关注了 Shahed Nasser 分享的关于多供应商市场的文章，他是一位非常出色的工程师。 但它只显示使用medusa-extender进行后端修改，不包括前端的更改。

所以我开始尝试弄清楚如何修改商店前端的部分，但首先我们需要为所有供应商定制原始API。

首先，遵循多供应商市场文章中的所有步骤。

其次，添加store.router.ts文件，如下所示。 在其中，我为所有供应商改进了客户端 API '/store/products'。

 import { MedusaAuthenticatedRequest, Router } from 'medusa-extender'; import { Response, NextFunction } from "express"; import { User } from '../../user/entities/user.entity'; @Router({ routes: [{ requiredAuth: false, path: '/store/products', method: 'get', handlers: [ async (req: MedusaAuthenticatedRequest, res: Response, next: NextFunction): Promise<Response<User[]>> => { const productService = req.scope.resolve("productService") const resProducts = await productService.list({}, { relations: [ "variants", "variants.prices", "variants.options", "options", "options.values", "images", "tags", "collection", "type", ], }); return res.send({ "products": resProducts, "count": resProducts.length, "offset": 0, "limit": 100 }); } ] }] }) export class StoreRouter {}

然后将StoreRouter添加到您的store.module.ts

 @Module({ imports: [Store, StoreRepository, StoreService, StoreRouter], })

跳过product.service.ts中的 loggedInUser 条件的简单方法，然后产品列表将更改为查询所有供应商。 这看起来很棘手，但我没有其他选择来使多供应商可行。

 prepareListQuery_(selector: object, config: object): object { /*const loggedInUser = this.container.loggedInUser if (loggedInUser) { selector['store_id'] = loggedInUser.store_id // this is for one store }*/ return super.prepareListQuery_(selector, config); }

在本地环境中重建并启动后端。 尝试通过邮递员创建新供应商。

对该供应商进行身份验证，然后创建一个新产品。

最后，所有的客户都可以查询到包括新厂商创建的新产品在内的所有产品，即使客户还没有登录。

困难的部分是商店前端的修改。 我在商店前端使用Medusa Gatsby Starter 。 但是，与我上面对后端的修改有很多不兼容的地方。

当我在生产环境中部署所有修改时，商店前端也重新构建。 但它显示失败如下：

最后发现gatsby的gatsby-source-filesystem中的create node函数（在构建时）不支持读取我在上一篇文章中使用的imgur的链接地址。

我手动将其更新为默认 s3 图像，然后成功重建。 但这不是最后一个任务。 如果产品没有任何上传的图像，我仍然在 gatsby-starter-medusa 中发现错误。
所以我们需要添加以下条件来防止这个错误。



原文:

Medusa is open-source project which provides many powerful e-commerce functions and extensions as Shopify. I follows the article for multi-vendor marketplaces shared from Shahed Nasser who is very outstanding engineer. But it only shows for backend modification with medusa-extender, not include the change of frontend side.

So I start to try to figure out how to modify the part of store frontend, but first we need to customize the original APIs for all vendors.

At first, following all steps from the article for multi-vendor marketplaces.

Second, add the store.router.ts file as below. In where, I refined the client API '/store/products' for all vendors.

import { MedusaAuthenticatedRequest, Router } from 'medusa-extender';
import { Response, NextFunction } from "express";
import { User } from '../../user/entities/user.entity';

@Router({
    routes: [{
        requiredAuth: false,
        path: '/store/products',
        method: 'get',
        handlers: [
            async (req: MedusaAuthenticatedRequest, res: Response, next: NextFunction): Promise<Response<User[]>> => {
                const productService = req.scope.resolve("productService")
                const resProducts = await productService.list({},
                    {
                        relations: [
                            "variants",
                            "variants.prices",
                            "variants.options",
                            "options",
                            "options.values",
                            "images",
                            "tags",
                            "collection",
                            "type",
                        ],
                    });
                return res.send({
                    "products": resProducts,
                    "count": resProducts.length,
                    "offset": 0,
                    "limit": 100
                });
            }
        ]
    }] 
})
export class StoreRouter {}

Then add StoreRouter into your store.module.ts

@Module({
    imports: [Store, StoreRepository, StoreService, StoreRouter],
})

Easy way to skip the loggedInUser condition in product.service.ts then the product list will be changed to query for all vendors. It looks like tricky but I didn't get other options to make multi-vendors workable.

    prepareListQuery_(selector: object, config: object): object {
        /*const loggedInUser = this.container.loggedInUser
        if (loggedInUser) {
            selector['store_id'] = loggedInUser.store_id // this is for one store
        }*/

        return super.prepareListQuery_(selector, config);
    }

Rebuild and start backend in your local environment. Try to create a new vendor by postman.

authenticate to this vendor then create a new product.

In the end, all clients can query all products including the new product created by the new vendor, even the client didn't login yet.

The difficult part is for the modification of store frontend. I use Medusa Gatsby Starter to my store frontend. However, there are many incompatible with my above modification of backend.

When I deployed all modification on the production environment, the store frontend rebuilt as well. But it shows failure as below:

Finally, I found the create node function(in the build time) from gatsby-source-filesystem of gatsby does not support to read the link address of imgur which I used in my previous article.

I manually updated it to default s3 images then it rebuilt successfully. But it is not the last mission. I still found an error in gatsby-starter-medusa if the product doesn't have any uploaded images.
So we need to add below condition to prevent from this error.
item.author: ivanchiou

item.title: web3 - metamask api 研究(1) | web3 - metamask api study(1)
item.description:
我正在尝试实现识别元掩码钱包的功能，并最终与应用程序连接，以便用户可以简单地注册或使用元掩码登录。 就像 opensea 注册/登录的工作方式一样。

我在MetaMask/detect-provider的 README.md 中关注此代码

最初，我正在使用 HTML/Javascript 模式（还不是 nodejs）来首先测试简单的功能。 并且 repo README 的示例 HTML 代码给出了错误。

正如它在博客中解释的那样，发生错误是因为它使用顶级等待而没有设置。 它需要更改package.json或 script 标签中的type= module 。

所以 README.md 中的 HTML 代码应该如下所示

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@metamask/detect-provider/dist/detect-provider.min.js"></script> <script type="module"> const provider = await detectEthereumProvider() if (provider) { // handle provider } else { // handle no provider } </script>



原文:

I'm trying to implement the feature that recognizes metamask wallet, and ultimately connects with the application so that the user can simply sign up or login with metamask login. Just like how opensea sign up / login works.

1. Original Code

I was following this code in the README.md of MetaMask/detect-provider

Initially, I'm working on HTML/Javascript mode (not nodejs yet) to test simple functions first. And the repo README's sample HTML code was giving an error.

2. error 

await is only valid in async functions and the top level bodies of modules

3. Solution

Fix - Await is only valid in async function Error in NodeJS

As it explained in the blog, the error occurred because it was using top level await without setting. It required to change type= module in package.json or script tag.

So the HTML code in the README.md should be like below

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@metamask/detect-provider/dist/detect-provider.min.js"></script>
<script type="module">
  const provider = await detectEthereumProvider()

  if (provider) {
    // handle provider
  } else {
    // handle no provider
  }
</script>
item.author: sosunnyproject

item.title: 如何设置顺风 | How to set up Tailwind
item.description:
本文介绍如何设置 Tailwind。

安装

npm i --save-dev tailwindcss

在里面

环境

// tailwind.config.js module . exports = { content : [ " ./src/**/*.{html,js} " ], theme : { extend : {}, }, plugins : [], };

写

/* src/index.css */ @tailwind base ; @tailwind components ; @tailwind utilities ;

建造

npx tailwindcss build -i ./src/index.css -o ./dist/index.css

在页面中使用 Tailwind

 <!-- src/index.html --> <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" /> <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> <link href= "/dist/index.css" rel= "stylesheet" /> </head> <body> <p class= "underline" > Hello </p> </body> </html>

查看

支持 Visual Studio 代码

// .vscode/extensions.json { "recommendations" : [ "bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss" ]
}


原文:

Tailwind is a utility-first CSS framework.
This article describes how to set up Tailwind.

Install 

npm i --save-dev tailwindcss

Init

Setting 

// tailwind.config.js module.exports = { content: ["./src/**/*.{html,js}"], theme: { extend: {}, }, plugins: [],
};

Write 

/* src/index.css */ @tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

Build 

npx tailwindcss build -i ./src/index.css -o ./dist/index.css

Using Tailwind in Page 

<!-- src/index.html --> <!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8" /> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> <link href="/dist/index.css" rel="stylesheet" /> </head> <body> <p class="underline">Hello</p> </body>
</html>

Check

http://localhost:3000/src/index.html

Support for Visual Studio Code 

// .vscode/extensions.json { "recommendations": ["bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss"]
}
item.author: jtakahashi64

item.title: 如何创建团队成员页面 - P2 - 使用 Meta Box 和 Oxygen | How to Create a Team Members Page - P2 - Using Meta Box and Oxygen
item.description:
要继续创建团队成员页面系列，我们将发布这篇文章来使用 Oxygen。 我们仍然使用自定义帖子类型而不是可克隆字段。 让我们看看如何！

在这个实践中，我也有与本系列第一个教程相同的页面示例，它使用了 Elementor。

视频版

开始之前

在深入研究这种做法之前，我们需要澄清一些事情。

与之前的 Elementor 教程一样，我也有包含成员信息的名片。 该信息以团队成员的自定义帖子类型保存在帖子中。

会员的姓名和头像是帖子的标题和特色图片。 其他信息，如职位、Facebook、Instagram 和邮件的链接将保存在不同的自定义字段中。

在这个实践中，我们需要Meta Box 核心插件有一个框架，允许您创建自定义字段来输入成员信息。 它是免费的，可在wordpress.org上找到。 对于高级功能，我们还需要对 Meta Box 进行一些扩展，如下所示：

最后一个是氧气。 您应该使用与 Meta Box 原生集成的 3.9 或更高版本。

第 1 步：创建新的自定义帖子类型

转到Meta Box > 帖子类型 > 新帖子类型以创建新的自定义帖子类型。

第 2 步：创建自定义字段

移动到Meta Box > Custom Fields > Add New 。

首先，为职位信息创建一个选择字段，然后在“选择”框中填写一些选项。 您可以像我一样填写选项或标签的值和标签。

如果要在管理员列中显示有关成员职位的信息，请在职位字段的设置中勾选选项显示为管理员列。 要进行此设置，您需要MB Admin Columns扩展。 注意为列设置位置，必须选择一个位置。 否则，它无法在任何地方显示。

我创建了URL字段，允许用户填写Facebook 、 Instagram和邮件信息的链接。

这就是我用来保存成员信息的所有自定义字段。

现在，移动到设置选项卡 >位置> 选择帖子类型作为团队成员，以将这些字段应用于此帖子类型。

返回帖子编辑器，您将看到创建的自定义字段。

第 3 步：为团队成员页面创建模板

在 Oxygen 中，创建一个新模板并选择inactive - Default Page以将其设置为继承此样式。

接下来，为团队成员创建一个新页面，并在“使用模板渲染页面”部分将创建的模板分配给该页面。

让我们编辑模板！

在“预览”部分中，选择要查看预览的页面。 然后，选择一个Section组件以包含所有团队成员的信息。

要自动获取页面标题，请添加Heading组件，然后单击Insert Data > Post > Title以将此组件与页面标题连接起来。

接下来，添加一个Repeater组件以从我们用于团队成员的所有帖子中获取数据。 要获取数据源，请转到查询 > 自定义 > 帖子类型，然后找到为我们的团队成员创建的帖子类型的 ID。

在下图中，有许多小框是我拥有的帖子数量。

如果您保留它，您将在下一次执行中看到所有帖子的预览。 但是，会乱套的。 为避免这种情况，请在“预览渲染”部分中，选择“单一模式”以仅查看帖子的预览。

在本教程中，成员的每张名片有 2 列。 一是显示图像和社交图标。 另一种是显示会员信息。 我将使用两个div标签将内容分成 2 列。

在第一个div标签中，添加Featured Image组件以将头像保存为帖子的特色图片。

接下来，为所有社交图标创建另一个div标签，以便在下一步中更轻松地设置它们的样式。

要创建社交图标，请选择Icon Button组件。 您知道社交链接也保存在自定义字段中。 因此，要将这些图标连接到我们需要的数据，请转到数据 > 元框字段> 选择相应的字段。

以Facebook图标为例：

然后，您可以根据需要自定义 Facebook 图标。

使用Instagram和邮件图标，也可以这样做。

现在，让我们在第二列中获取成员的信息。

选择标题组件以显示成员的姓名，因为它是帖子标题。

要获取成员的位置，请添加Text组件。 由于每个成员的位置也保存在自定义字段中，因此将文本连接到位置字段，就像我们对社交图标所做的那样。 单击“插入数据”按钮后，选择“元框字段”>“位置” 。

由于Position是一个选择字段，因此选择框中的选项可能只有我在步骤 2 中设置的标签，或者像往常一样包含值和标签。 在使用 Oxygen 编辑模板时，当您获取位置信息时，也有两个选项。 而且，我会选择标签。

最后，选择Content组件以简要介绍该成员。

因此，在 Meet the Team 页面上，您可以看到有关成员的所有信息。

让我们为这个页面设置样式。

第 4 步：设置团队成员页面的样式

要设置此页面的样式，您可以选择每个组件并更改设置。

在“高级”选项卡中，有很多选择，您可以根据需要设置样式。

在为组件设置样式后，要查看所有帖子，只需将Preview Render部分启用为Normal Mode 。

你看到所有的盒子都粘在一起了。 因此，我需要在每个框之间添加一些间距。 因此，转到网格布局部分并单击启用网格按钮。

现在，所有盒子的展示都相当不错。 所以我只保留它。 如果您想更改任何内容，只需更改左侧边栏中的设置即可。

最后，我想要页面的标题在中心。 我试图用氧气设计它，但它没有用。 让我们尝试一些 CSS 来进行更改。

转到Manage > Stylesheets > New Stylesheet ，然后添加此代码。

 .meet-the-team-section .ct-section-inner-wrap { .meet-the-team-section .ct-section-inner-wrap {
对齐项目：未设置；
让我们看看结果！
 
 最后的话
我们已经完成了使用 Meta Box 和 Oxygen 创建团队成员页面的所有步骤。 尝试一下并在评论部分分享结果。 如果您使用其他页面构建器，请关注我们的频道以查看其他教程。


原文:

To continue the series Create Team Members Page, we’re having this post to do it with Oxygen. We still use a custom post type instead of cloneable fields. Let’s see how!

In this practice, I also have the same sample of the page as the first tutorial in this series which uses Elementor.

Video Version

https://youtu.be/CgI4bxfOkk4

Before Getting Started

Before going deeply into this practice, there is something that we need to clarify.

As in the previous tutorial for Elementor, I also have name cards to include the members’ information. That information is saved in posts in a custom post type for the team members.

The member’s name and avatar are the title and featured image of the post. Other information such as position, links to Facebook, Instagram, and Mail will be saved in different custom fields.

In this practice, we need the Meta Box core plugin to have a framework that allows you to create custom fields to input members’ information. It’s free and available on wordpress.org. For advanced features, we also need some extensions of Meta Box as follows:

The last one is Oxygen. You should use the 3.9 version or higher which has the native integration with Meta Box.

Step 1: Create a New Custom Post Type

Go to Meta Box > Post Types > New Post Type to create a new custom post type.

Step 2: Create Custom Fields

Move to Meta Box > Custom Fields > Add New.

First, create a select field for the position information then fill in some options into the Choices box. You can fill in both value and label for the options or the label only as I did.

If you want to display the information about the members’ position in the admin columns, tick the option Show as an admin column in the Position field’s settings. To have this setting, you need the MB Admin Columns extension. Pay attention to the set the position for the column, you must choose a position. Otherwise, it can not be displayed anywhere.

I create URL fields that allows users to fill in a link for the Facebook, Instagram, and mail information.

That’s all the custom fields I use to save the members’ information.

Now, move to the Settings tab > Location > choose Post Type as Team Member to apply these fields to this post type.

Back to the post editor, you will see the created custom fields.

Step 3: Create a Template for the Team Members Page

In the Oxygen, create a new template and choose inactive - Default Page to set it to inherit this style.

Next, create a new page for the team members and assign the created template to this page in the Render Page Using Template section.

Let’s edit the template!

In the Previewing section, choose the page you want to see the preview. Then, select a Section component to contain all of the team members’ information.

To get the title of the page automatically, add the Heading component, then click Insert Data > Post > Title to connect this component with the title of the page.

Next, add a Repeater component to get the data from all the posts that we use for our team members. To get the source of data, go to Query > Custom > Post Type and find the ID of the created post type for our team members.

In the below image, there are many small boxes which are the number of posts that I have.

If you keep it, you will see the preview of all the posts in the next executions. But, it’ll be messed up. To avoid that, in the Preview Render section, choose Single Mode to see the preview of a post only.

In this tutorial, each name card of a member has 2 columns. One is to display the image and social icons. Another one is to show the member information. I'll use two div tags to divide the content into 2 columns.

In the first div tag, add the Featured Image component to have the avatar saved as the post's featured image.

Next, create another div tag for all social icons in order to style them easier in the next step.

To create the social icons, choose the Icon Button component. You know that the social links are also saved in custom fields. So, to connect these icons to the data we need, go to data > Meta Box Field > choose the corresponding field.

Take the Facebook icon as an example:

Then, you can customize the Facebook icon as you want.

With the Instagram and mail icons, do likewise.

Now, let’s get the member’s information in the second column.

Choose the Title component for displaying the member's name since it is the post title.

To get the member's position, add the Text component. Since the position of each member is also saved in a custom field, connect the text to the position field like the way we did with the social icons. After clicking the Insert Data button, choose Meta Box Field > Position.

Since the Position is a select field, the options in the choices box may have only labels as I set in step 2, or include both value and label as normal. In the template editing with Oxygen, when you get the position’s information, there are two options as well. And, I’ll choose the label.

Finally, choose the Content component to get a short introduction of the member.

So, on the Meet the Team page, you can see all the information about the members.

Let’s style this page.

Step 4: Style the Team Members Page

To style this page, you can choose each component and change the settings.

In the Advanced tab, there are a lot of choices so that you can style as you want.

After styling the components, to see all the posts, just enable the Preview Render section to the Normal Mode.

You see that all the boxes are stuck together. Therefore, I need to add some spacing between each box. Thus, go to the Grid layout section and click the Enable Grid button.

Now, the display of all the boxes is quite good. So I just keep it. If you want to change anything, just change settings in the left sidebar.

Finally, I want the title of the page in the center. I tried to style it with Oxygen but it didn’t work. Let’s try some CSS to make a change.

Go to Manage > Stylesheets > New Stylesheet, then add this code.

.meet-the-team-section .ct-section-inner-wrap {
    align-items: unset;
}

Let’s see the result!

Last Words

We’ve completed all the steps to create a team members page with Meta Box and Oxygen. Try it out and share the results in the comment section. If you use another page builder, keep track of our channel to see other tutorials.
